pitch.title: Final Girl Therapy
pitch.logline: A cynical support group of horror movie survivors must band together when a new, genre-savvy serial killer crashes their secluded wellness retreat.
pitch.core_theme: Overcoming past trauma and breaking out of expected roles.
pitch.synopsis: Act I: Chloe, a survivor of a summer camp massacre, reluctantly joins a wellness retreat for other final survivors. They spend their days swapping trauma stories and mocking horror tropes. Act II: A masked killer arrives, using their specific tropes against them. The survivors realize they cannot rely on their old survival tactics because the killer knows them all. Chloe steps up to lead, forcing the group to act like the first victims to confuse the killer. Act III: In a chaotic finale, the survivors turn the retreat into a trope-defying trap, unmasking the killer as their seemingly meek therapist who wanted to create the ultimate sequel.

beats.description: Chloe wakes from a hyper-violent summer camp nightmare, showcasing her severe PTSD and inability to move past her trauma.

Chloe reluctantly arrives at the secluded Pine Haven wellness retreat, meeting the meek therapist Dr. Vance and a cynical group of fellow Final Girls who immediately begin swapping survival stories and mocking horror tropes.
The group refuses to engage in actual therapy, instead bonding over their shared hyper-vigilance and pointing out all the structural flaws of the retreat's security.
The retreat's lone groundskeeper is found dead in a stylized, classic slasher setup, therefore confirming a new killer has trapped them in the woods without communication.
The Final Girls instantly snap into their respective veteran survival modes, effortlessly barricading doors and fashioning makeshift weapons.
The masked killer attacks but perfectly predicts their veteran maneuvers, using their specific franchise tropes against them and nearly slaughtering the entire group.
Retreating into the woods, the group realizes their battle-tested tactics are now fatal liabilities because the killer has studied all their movies.
Chloe steps up as the definitive leader, proposing a radical survival plan: they must act like clueless, doomed first-act victims to completely disrupt the killer's narrative expectations.
The hardened survivors awkwardly force themselves to split up, drink inappropriately, and loudly investigate strange noises in the dark.
The killer becomes deeply confused and frustrated by their bizarre, illogical behavior, which successfully causes the killer to hesitate and miss a massive ambush.
Furious at the broken rules, the killer abandons classic slasher tropes and pivots to brutal, unpredictable modern horror tactics.
The killer violently breaches their safe room, seemingly killing the group's toughest survivor and cornering Chloe, forcing her to relive the exact summer camp trauma she has been running from.
Chloe refuses to be a victim again, therefore she rallies the remaining survivors to stop running and weaponize the retreat's layout against the killer.
The survivors lure the killer into the main lodge using perfectly executed anti-tropes, trapping the murderer in a heavily fortified, inescapable dead-end.
The group subdues the killer, unmasking them as Dr. Vance, who maniacally monologues about orchestrating the ultimate sequel to cure their trauma through extreme exposure therapy.
Chloe interrupts the villain's monologue with a genre-defying one-liner, neutralizes Vance permanently, and leads the surviving women out of the woods, finally healed and ready to live normal lives.

characters.name: Chloe
characters.brief_summary: A deeply traumatized survivor of a summer camp massacre who hides her severe PTSD behind biting cynicism. She serves as the reluctant leader who must learn to break the 'Final Girl' mold to save herself and her peers.

psychological_core.wound: She survived her original massacre while all her friends died, leaving her with crushing survivor's guilt.
psychological_core.false_belief: I only survived by being an isolated, paranoid victim, and I can never function as a normal person again.
psychological_core.fear: Taking on a leadership role and being responsible for other people's deaths a second time.
psychological_core.desire: To feel genuinely safe and finally get a single good night of sleep.
psychological_core.need: To let go of her survivor's guilt, trust others, and take proactive control of her own narrative.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Uses dark humor, morbid sarcasm, and clinical horror terminology to distance herself from genuine emotion. She NEVER says anything optimistic like 'We are going to be fine' or offers false hope to anyone.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: She points out the logistical or tactical flaws in someone's argument, or critiques the room's physical security, instead of addressing the emotional weight of the conversation.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Chloe appears highly competent and casually sarcastic 90 percent of the time, merely double-checking exits or sitting facing the door to mask her hyper-vigilance. Her raw trauma only breaks through in the 10 percent of moments when she is forced to make a decision that puts someone else in danger, causing her voice to crack and her to lash out aggressively.

characters.name: Dr. Vance
characters.brief_summary: A seemingly mild-mannered, empathetic therapist who runs the Pine Haven wellness retreat. In reality, he is a deranged horror-fanatic obsessed with curing trauma through extreme, cinematic exposure therapy.

psychological_core.wound: He was a helpless, ignored victim of his own past trauma who felt empowered only by watching horror movie survivors overcome their monsters.
psychological_core.false_belief: True psychological healing can only be achieved by facing imminent death and recreating the cinematic crucible of survival.
psychological_core.fear: Being ordinary, forgettable, and entirely ineffective in his psychiatric practice.
psychological_core.desire: To orchestrate the ultimate real-life sequel and go down in history as a mastermind who successfully 'fixed' Final Girls.
psychological_core.need: To realize his god-complex is just a pathetic mask for his own untreated cowardice and lack of control.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in soothing, overly-measured therapeutic buzzwords like 'holding space' and 'validating your journey'. He NEVER uses vulgarity, raises his voice, or interrupts someone else's sentence during normal conversation.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: He turns the question back on the patient with a gentle, probing inquiry, such as 'And how does that make you feel?' or 'Why do you think you are asking me that?'

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Vance plays the perfectly harmless, slightly pathetic beta-male therapist 90 percent of the time, using his meekness to make the women drop their guard. Only 10 percent of the time, specifically when someone accidentally breaks a horror trope or insults the slasher genre, does a terrifying flash of intense, rigid, and controlling fanaticism bleed through his soft-spoken demeanor.

characters.name: Riley
characters.brief_summary: A former suburban babysitter who survived a Halloween night massacre and now overcompensates with hyper-aggressive, weapon-hoarding paranoia. She acts as the group's muscle and primary skeptic of the 'play dumb' strategy.

psychological_core.wound: The children she was babysitting were killed while she was hiding in a closet.
psychological_core.false_belief: Violence and physical aggression are the only guaranteed ways to prevent victimization.
psychological_core.fear: Being helpless, unarmed, and failing to protect the innocent ever again.
psychological_core.desire: To kill the monster before it even has a chance to strike, proving she is no longer weak.
psychological_core.need: To forgive herself for the past and realize true strength is not just about wielding a weapon.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Speaks in clipped, aggressive, tactical jargon and swears frequently. She NEVER says 'I am sorry' or admits to being afraid of anything.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: She instigates a physical task like sharpening a knife, checking ammunition, or barricading a door to avoid answering a personal question.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Riley comes off as a gym-obsessed, slightly intense tomboy 90 percent of the time, projecting an aura of untouchable confidence. Only 10 percent of the time, specifically when children or hiding spots are mentioned, does her aggressive facade crack, revealing a frantic, trembling desperation to maintain control.

characters.name: Lexi
characters.brief_summary: A former sorority sister who survived a campus slasher by sheer dumb luck and masks her imposter syndrome with superficiality. She serves as the group's unwilling bait and unexpected tactical genius when playing dumb.

psychological_core.wound: She only survived her massacre because she was passed out drunk while her braver sisters fought and died.
psychological_core.false_belief: I am a fake Final Girl who does not deserve to be alive or in this support group.
psychological_core.fear: The other survivors finding out she is a fraud and abandoning her to die.
psychological_core.desire: To prove she is genuinely smart and worthy of the survivor title.
psychological_core.need: To accept that survival is not a meritocracy and that her life still has value.

voice_and_behavior.speech_patterns: Uses vocal fry, upspeak, and rapid-fire pop-culture references, talking extremely fast when nervous. She NEVER talks about the actual night of her survival in any specific detail.
voice_and_behavior.deflection_tactic: She changes the subject to mundane gossip, fashion, or complains about the retreat's lack of amenities like Wi-Fi and good coffee.

characters.subtlety_guidelines: Lexi acts like a vapid, self-absorbed influencer who accidentally stumbled into a trauma group 90 percent of the time. Only 10 percent of the time, when someone praises her bravery or directly asks how she defeated her killer, does she completely freeze, her eyes darting in panic as she desperately searches for an excuse to leave the room.
